tag_id.comments: unique tag identifier in the IMOS Animal Tracking Acoustic Telemetry Database

transmitter_deployment_id.comments: unique identifier for the deployment of the tag in the IMOS Animal Tracking Acoustic Telemetry Database

tag_deployment_project_name.comments: Project name which deployed the tag in the IMOS Animal Tracking Acoustic Telemetry Database

CAAB_species_id.comments: Codes for Australian Aquatic Biota (CAAB) species identifier

WORMS_species_aphia_id.comments: World Register of Marine Species (WoRMS) Aphia identifier

detection_datetime.comments: Timestamp of the detection in UTC

receiver_project_name.comments: Project name which deployed the receiver in the IMOS Animal Tracking Acoustic Telemetry Database

installation_name.comments: Installation name where the receiver was deployed in the IMOS Animal Tracking Acoustic Telemetry Database

station_name.comments: Station name within the installation where the receiver was deployed in the IMOS Animal Tracking Acoustic Telemetry Database

receiver_name.comments: Receiver name combines the receiver model and the receiver serial number

receiver_id.comments: Unique receiver identifier in the IMOS Animal Tracking Acoustic Telemetry Database

receiver_deployment_id.comments: Unique identifier for the deployment of the receiver in the IMOS Animal Tracking Acoustic Telemetry Database

receiver_status.comments: Status of the receiver (e.g. deployed, recovered, lost, etc.) in the IMOS Animal Tracking Acoustic Telemetry Database

receiver_deployment_datetime.comments: Timestamp of the receiver deployment in UTC

receiver_recovery_datetime.comments: Timestamp of the receiver recovery in UTC

tag_device_project_name.comments: Project name which deployed the tag device in the IMOS Animal Tracking Acoustic Telemetry Database

transmitter_sensor_type.comments: Type of sensor on the transmitter (e.g. temperature, pressure, etc.)

transmitter_sensor_raw_value.comments: Raw sensor values (unitless numbers, usually between 1 and 255) that require an equation for conversion into familiar units: sensor_value = (transmitter_sensor_raw_value * transmitter_sensor_slope) + transmitter_sensor_intercept

transmitter_sensor_unit.comments: Unit corresponding to the sensor on the transmitter

transmitter_sensor_slope.comments: Transmitter sensor slope used to convert raw sensor values into familiar units: sensor_value = (transmitter_sensor_raw_value * transmitter_sensor_slope) + transmitter_sensor_intercept

transmitter_sensor_intercept.comments: Transmitter sensor intercept used to convert raw sensor values into familiar units: sensor_value = (transmitter_sensor_raw_value * transmitter_sensor_slope) + transmitter_sensor_intercept

transmitter_type.comments: Tag model (e.g. V9, V13, V16, etc.) of the transmitter in the IMOS Animal Tracking Acoustic Telemetry Database

transmitter_status.comments: Status of the transmitter in the IMOS Animal Tracking Acoustic Telemetry Database

transmitter_deployment_datetime.comments: Timestamp of the transmitter deployment in UTC

transmitter_dual_sensor.comments: TRUE if the transmitter has two sensors, FALSE if it has one sensor

active.comments: True if the transmitter is still active, FALSE if it is inactive

placement.comments: Surgery placement of the transmitter (e.g. internal, external, etc.)

transmitter_recovery_datetime.comments: Timestamp of the transmitter recovery in UTC if recovered, otherwise NULL

transmitter_recovery_latitude.comments: Transmitter recovery latitude (WGS 84 datum) if recovered, otherwise NULL

transmitter_recovery_longitude.comments: Transmitter recovery longitude (WGS 84 datum) if recovered, otherwise NULL

measurement.comments: Measurement type of the animal tagged with its value and unit

FDA_QC.comments: detection fails this test (‘FDA_QC’ = 2) if tag detected only once or if there were more long (> 12 hours) than short periods (< 30 minutes) between detections at a given installation. Quality Control Flag Values: ## 0: no qc performed ## 1: good data ## 2: likely invalid data ## 3: missing data

Velocity_QC.comments: a given detection failed this test if the velocity with the previous and next receiver was greater than 10 m.s-1 (‘Velocity_QC’ = 2). Quality Control Flag Values: ## 0: no qc performed ## 1: good data ## 2: likely invalid data ## 3: missing data

Distance_QC.comments: a given detection failed this test if the distance with the previous and next receiver was greater than 1000 km (‘Distance_QC’ = 2). Quality Control Flag Values: ## 0: no qc performed ## 1: good data ## 2: likely invalid data ## 3: missing data

DetectionDistribution_QC.comments: detections failed this test if they occurred outside of a species known occurrence area (‘DetectionDistribution_QC’ = 2), allowing for uncertainties in compiled distributions and climate-induced species range shifts by extending the original area’s latitudinal range by 500 km. Quality Control Flag Values: ## 0: no qc performed ## 1: good data ## 2: likely invalid data ## 3: missing data

DistanceRelease_QC.comments: a given detection passed this test (‘DistanceRelease_QC’ = 1) if it occurred within a 500 km radius of where the tagged animal was released. Quality Control Flag Values: ## 0: no qc performed ## 1: good data ## 2: likely invalid data ## 3: missing data

ReleaseDate_QC.comments: detections failed this test (‘ReleaseDate_QC’ = 2) if they occurred before a tag’s release date. Quality Control Flag Values: ## 0: no qc performed ## 1: good data ## 2: likely invalid data ## 3: missing data

ReleaseLocation_QC.comments: tag's release coordinates failed this test (‘ReleaseLocation’ = 2) if falling outside of a species distribution area and/or farther than 500 km of the first detection. Quality Control Flag Values: ## 0: no qc performed ## 1: good data ## 2: likely invalid data ## 3: missing data

Detection_QC.comments: this field summarises the output of the five first tests, i.e. ‘FDA_QC’, ‘Distance_QC’, ‘Velocity_QC’, ‘DetectionDistribution_QC’, and ‘DistanceRelease_QC’: ## 1: valid detection - All five tests passed ## 2: probably valid detection - Four tests passed ## 3: probably bad detection - Three tests passed ## 4: bad detection - Two or less tests passed

aws_opendata_registry.Name: IMOS - Animal Tracking Facility - Acoustic Tracking - Quality Controlled Detections (2007 - ongoing)
aws_opendata_registry.Description:
Since 2007, the Integrated Marine Observing System’s Animal Tracking Facility (formerly known as the Australian Animal Tracking And Monitoring System (AATAMS)) has established a permanent array of acoustic receivers around Australia to detect the movements of tagged marine animals in coastal waters. Simultaneously, the Animal Tracking Facility developed a centralised national database (https://animaltracking.aodn.org.au/) to encourage collaborative research across the Australian research community and provide unprecedented opportunities to monitor broad-scale animal movements. The resulting dataset comprises observations of tagged animals in Australian waters collected by IMOS infrastructure as well as receivers operated by independent research projects and  organisations. This dataset constitutes a valuable resource facilitating meta-analysis of animal movement, distributions, and habitat use, and is important for relating species distribution shifts with environmental covariates.

This dataset comprises all available (2007 – ongoing) quality-controlled and publicly accessible animal detections collected across the collaborative, continental IMOS network for a range of aquatic species (fish, sharks, rays, reptiles, and mammals). Here, raw animal detections collated via the IMOS Australian Animal Acoustic Telemetry Database have been quality-controlled as per Hoenner et al. (2018). This dataset is updated on a six-monthly basis.

Note - There is a static snapshot of the database (up until 2017) (http://dx.doi.org/10.4225/69/5979810a7dd6f), and this has been documented in a Scientific Data Publication (Hoenner et al. 2018).
